dairy-free-almond-joy-hot-chocolate.name: Dairy-free Almond Joy® Hot Chocolate
instructions: Stir coconut-almond milk blend, cocoa powder, maple syrup, brown sugar, vanilla extract, and salt together in a saucepan over medium heat; stir with a whisk to dissolve solids while the mixture heats to your desired temperature.

dairy-free-honey-pumpkin-pie.name: Dairy-free Honey Pumpkin Pie
instructions: Heat oven to 204°C.

In medium bowl, mix flour and 1/4 teaspoon salt. With pastry blender or 2 knives, cut shortening into flour until texture is crumbly. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons of the almondmilk over mixture and mix with fork until mixture comes together in a ball. If necessary, sprinkle a little more almondmilk over the mixture.

Sprinkle a little flour over a 12-inch square of waxed paper. Place ball of dough in center; pat into disc. Sprinkle with flour and top with another square of waxed paper. Roll very thinly into 12-inch circle. Remove top piece of waxed paper and turn crust upside down over a 9-inch deep-dish pie plate. Fit into pie plate and remove waxed paper. Crimp edges.
To make the filling, beat the rest of the ingredients and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt with electric mixer on low speed in a large bowl until well-mixed. Pour into crust.

Bake 50 to 55 minutes or until knife inserted 1 inch from edge comes out clean. Cover edges of crust with strips of foil to keep from over-browning, if necessary. Cool completely, about 2 hours. Store in refrigerator.

dark-greens-and-noodles-with-yogurt.name: Dark Greens And Noodles With Yogurt

ingredients.prep: or all-purpose flour, plus more to dust
or 3 0.5 ounces each nettles, wild garlic, and spinach

instructions: Pour the measured water into a large bowl and add the egg. Whisk it in well. Then add the flour, mixing it with your hand thoroughly into a soft dough. Sprinkle a work surface with more flour and knead the dough until it stops sticking to your hands and is smooth. Leave it to rest, covered, for at least 30 minutes, or up to 24 hours in the fridge overnight.
You won’t need all the caramelized onions in this recipe, only half. But there is no point doing this in smaller quantities, also the onions are so good you can use them in a myriad of other dishes.

So, cut the onions in half and slice finely along the grain. Melt 2 tablespoons (30 g) of the clarified butter, or use a pat of regular butter and a splash of oil. Add the onions and a generous pinch of salt. Cover with a lid if you have one, or with a cartouche (a round piece of parchment paper), and let them sweat over medium-low heat for 20 minutes. They will become soft and translucent. Now uncover, increase the heat to medium, and cook for another 10 minutes, stirring from time to time. If they become dry and start catching, add splashes of water and deglaze. See what they are like after the 10 minutes, chances are you will need another 5–10 minutes of cooking. You want them to be deeply amber and caramelized.

Take half the rested dough and mold it into a flattish disc with your hands. Roll it out over a well-floured surface into a rough circle, 18 inches in diameter if you can, but no need to go overboard and make it see-through or anything. Roll it up around your floured rolling pin. Slash lengthways with a knife, once, along the pin, without dragging the knife. The dough will drop to the surface in layers of dough strips. Cut them in half lengthways, and then on the diagonal at 2½ inches (6 cm) intervals. You will have lots of diamond-shaped pieces of pasta. Put them on a floured tray so they don’t stick together. Repeat with the other half of the dough.

If the pasta diamonds are not to be used immediately, you can freeze them to be used another time. If you freeze them, again make sure they are well-floured so they don’t merge and form a giant pasta monster.

If you are using nettles, have a bowl with cold water and ice ready. Put them into a saucepan and cover with boiling water, boil for 3 minutes, then take out with tongs or a spider strainer and plunge into the iced water. Now that the nettles are disarmed, cut off any of the thick stalks and chop across the leaves.
Now add the nettles and sliced wild garlic and spinach to a pan and cook over medium-low heat in the remaining clarified butter for 5–10 minutes.

If using other greens, there’s no need to blanch them, just put them into a pan sliced and raw with the remaining clarified butter. Season well with salt and pepper and cook until they look soft and wilted (kale will take longer than spinach). Mix together the yogurt and garlic in a bowl.

Cook the pasta in well-salted water for 2–3 minutes. Then drain, but not too vigorously, you want a little bit of the pasta water. Put them into the pan with the cooked greens, add half of the caramelized onions (save the rest for later) and mix them through thoroughly as well. Put everything on a platter, dot garlicky yogurt around, and sprinkle with sumac and crispy breadcrumbs.

delicious-apple-streusel-cheesecake.name: Delicious Apple Streusel Cheesecake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9-inch springform pan.
Make crust: Mix together graham cracker crumbs, sugar, melted butter, and cinnamon in a medium bowl until well combined. Press into the bottom of the prepared springform pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden, 7 to 9 minutes. Cool to room temperature, 15 to 20 minutes.
While crust cools, make batter: Mix together apples, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a medium bowl until evenly coated. Set aside.

Beat together cream cheese and white sugar in a large bowl until smooth. Slowly add milk. Beat in eggs one at a time. Stir in sour cream, flour, and vanilla on low speed or by hand until batter is smooth. Do not overmix; small bits of cream cheese are okay.
Pour 1/2 of the batter over the prepared crust. Arrange coated apples over filling, leaving a 1/2-inch border. Top with remaining batter.
Bake in the preheated oven for 40 minutes.
Meanwhile, make topping: Combine flour, brown sugar, and melted butter in a small bowl until well combined.

Sprinkle topping quickly over cheesecake. Continue baking until set, about 20 minutes more. Turn the oven off but do not open the door, allowing cheesecake to cool in the oven for at least 4 hours; this helps prevent cracking. Refrigerate to allow cheesecake to fully set before serving, 2 to 4 hours more.

delicious-chocolate-cake-with-peanut-but.name: Delicious Chocolate with Peanut Butter
delicious-chocolate-cake-with-peanut-but.original_name: Deliciosa tarta de chocolate con mantequilla de cacahuete
instructions: Triple the cookies till they get pilled.
With the help of a mortar or processor, tighten the peanut.
Drop butter and mix with cookies and shredded peanuts.
Expand the mixture into a round plate until cover. Refrigerate in the refrigerator while continuing with the recipe.
It hydrates Jelatine in water for 7 minutes.
Warm up his milk till it's warm. Then add the hydrated and well-drained jelly leaves. Mix till integrated.
In a bowl, add mascarpona, glass sugar and peanut butter. Bat with an electrical mixer till integrated.
Once ready, add milk and jelly to the earlier mixture and integrate.
Fold this mixture into the cookie base and refrigerate for about 2 hours.
When there's a refrigerator, heat up the cream till it's a little hot and add the chocolate tablets till they melt. Let them stand.
Once at temperature, pour the mixture into the plate and refrigerate to cover for 2 hours.
When she's curved, she'll be ready to have you. Cut into portions and enjoy.

delicious-sweet-and-sour-peppers-and-fet.name: Delicious Sweet And Sour Peppers And Feta Cheese Toast
delicious-sweet-and-sour-peppers-and-fet.original_name: Deliciosa tostadas de pimientos agridulces y queso feta
instructions: Take the peppers and cut them into small or brunoise cubes.
Once you have the cut vegetables, heat up a frying pan with olive oil.
When the frying pan is hot, it incorporates the peppers and pits for 3 minutes till they have some transparency.
After that time, she incorporates into the frying pan vinegar chorrit, brown sugar, salt and curry to taste.
Mezcla y deja cocinar por 12-15 minutos aproximadamente.
When the peppers are cooking, get the bread toast. Wet the toast till they get crispy.
When you notice that the peppers are caramelized, turn off.
Take the toast, put with the caramelized peppers and finally spread the Feta cheese.
That's it. They'll be perfect to eat. Enjoy!

delicious-tapa-with-shrimp-comedera.name: Delicious Tapa With Shrimp
instructions: Begins the recipe, takes the shrimp and removes the vein at the centre. Then cut them into half to make them smaller and be ideal for tapas.
Pela y filetea finamente los dientes de ajo.
Trocea el perejil y reserva.
Warm a frying pan or pan and add butter.
Once melting, it incorporates the steamed garlic and pits with medium heat for 2 minutes and keeps moving. Be sure they don't get burned.
Posteriormente, incorpora los camarones picados y condimenta con sal y pimienta. Mezcla y saltea por 5 minutos hasta que se cocinen.
Fold the lemon juice and the chopped parsley. Mix and finish cooking for another 2 minutes, get juicy. Turn it off.
Once ready the shrimp, get the bread toast and put up a portion of the shrimp. Space more parsley or lemon juice.
Enjoy!

devils-float.name: Devil's Float
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Sift 3/4 cup white sugar, flour, 2 tablespoons cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt together in a bowl. Stir in milk, butter, and vanilla extract until just combined; fold in pecans. Pour batter into an 8x8-inch baking dish.
Combine remaining 1/2 cup white sugar, brown sugar, and remaining 2 tablespoons cocoa powder in a bowl; sprinkle over batter. Pour hot water over top.
Bake in the preheated oven until top cake layer is floating on bottom pudding layer, about 40 minutes.

doro-wat-ethiopian-chicken-dish.name: Doro Wat: Ethiopian Chicken Dish
instructions: Melt 1/2 cup butter in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add onion; cook and stir until translucent, 5 to 6 minutes. Add 1/2 cup water and tomato paste; stir until hot, about 2 minutes. Stir in berbere, remaining 1/2 cup butter, garlic, and ginger. Reduce heat to low; cook until mixture thickens to a paste consistency, 20 to 30 minutes.
Stir remaining 2 cups water into berbere paste; add chicken. Simmer until thickened to sauce consistency, about 45 minutes.
Stir wine, cardamom, and black pepper into sauce; add hard-cooked eggs. Cook until sauce is slightly reduced, about 15 minutes more.

double-layer-pumpkin-cheesecake.name: Double-layer Pumpkin Cheesecake
instructions: To make the cheesecake layer: Beat cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla in a large bowl with an electric mixer until smooth. Add eggs, one at a time, blending well after each addition.
Spread 1 cup batter in the graham cracker crust.
To make the pumpkin layer: Add pumpkin puree, cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg to the remaining batter; stir gently until well blended.
Carefully spread on top of plain cheesecake batter in the crust.
Bake in the preheated oven until the edges are puffed and the surface is firm except for a small spot in the center that jiggled when the pan is gently shaken, 35 to 40 minutes.
Remove from the oven, set on a wire rack, and cool to room temperature, 1 to 2 hours.
Refrigerate until completely set, at least 3 hours or overnight before slicing.

duck-a-lorange.name: Duck À L'orange
instructions: Set giblets from duck aside; reserve liver for another use. Trim skin from both openings of the cavity; set aside for rendering. Cut both leg-thigh pieces off duck. Cut wings from breasts. Carve breasts from carcass for 2 boneless breasts. Trim off wing tips and flats; set aside. Cut carcass into quarters. Cover and chill leg, breast, and wing pieces.

Heat reserved duck skin in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Cook, stirring, until about 2 Tbsp. fat is rendered; discard skin. Add giblets, wing tips and flats, carcass pieces, onion, and thyme sprigs. Cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned, about 15 minutes. Add celery, carrot, peppercorns, and 6 cups water. Bring to a boil over high heat, skimming any foam that rises to the surface. Reduce heat to medium; simmer duck stock for 1 hour.

Meanwhile, using a sharp knife, cut all peel and white pith from 1 orange; set peel aside. Working over a medium bowl, cut between membranes to release segments into bowl; set aside. Add orange peel to simmering stock. Juice remaining 2 oranges and set juice aside.

Strain stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a medium bowl (you should have about 2 cups). Transfer orange peel to a work surface; discard remaining solids in sieve. Slice peel into thin strips (remove white pith for a more refined look, if desired); set aside. DO AHEAD Stock can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill stock and peel separately.

Prick duck pieces all over with a sharp knife. Season with salt and pepper. Arrange them in a single layer in a large heavy skillet (use 2 if needed), skin side down, and cook over medium heat, occasionally pouring off fat from skillet into a heatproof bowl, until skin is deep golden brown, 18–20 minutes. (Cover and chill fat; save for cooking potatoes or stir-fried rice.)

Turn over duck pieces. Cook breasts, skin side up, until an instant-read thermometer inserted horizontally into thickest part of breast registers 125° for medium-rare, about 2 minutes. Transfer breasts to a large rimmed baking sheet. Continue cooking legs and wings until well browned on both sides and juices run clear when thigh is pierced with a fork, 10–15 minutes longer. Transfer to sheet with breasts. Pour off fat from skillet, leaving browned bits behind.

Preheat oven to 450°. Return skillet with browned bits to medium-high heat. Pour in Port and cook, scraping pan with a wooden spoon (the browned bits will add extra flavor to the sauce), until reduced and syrupy, about 2 minutes. Add reserved orange juice and duck stock; boil, stirring occasionally, until sauce is smooth and thickened, about 20 minutes. Season sauce to taste with salt and pepper. Cover and keep warm.
Place rimmed baking sheet with duck pieces in oven. Bake until duck is just heated through, 5–8 minutes.
Pour warm sauce onto a deep platter. Scatter orange segments and strips of peel over. Top with duck pieces.

dunton-hot-springs-granola.name: Dunton Hot Springs Granola
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°. Toss oats, cashews, pecans, pumpkin seeds, coconut, oil, maple syrup, groats, honey, almonds, sunflower seeds, flaxseeds, sesame seeds, vanilla extract, and salt in a large bowl. Spread out on a parchment-lined baking sheet and bake, stirring occasionally, until golden brown, 50–60 minutes. Let granola cool on pan, then break into clusters.
DO AHEAD: Granola can be made 2 weeks ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.
